title.en: Minimum removals to eliminate overlapping intervals
title.es: Mínimas eliminaciones para quitar intervalos solapados

statement.en:
Given `int[][] intervals` where each row is `{start, end}` (both inclusive), return the **minimum number of intervals to remove** so that no two remaining intervals overlap.

Signature: `public int eraseOverlapIntervals(int[][] intervals)`

**Overlap definition:** Two intervals `[a, b]` and `[c, d]` overlap when `c < b` (their interiors intersect). Intervals that share only an endpoint, like `[1, 2]` and `[2, 3]`, do **NOT** overlap — the shared point is acceptable.

**Key insight — greedy by end time:** Sort by `end` ascending. Greedily keep each interval whose `start` is ≥ the `end` of the last kept interval. Every interval that cannot be kept must be removed. Counting removals is equivalent to `n − (maximum non-overlapping subset size)`.

**Sort key:** `end` ascending. Tie-breaking on `end` is arbitrary — the count does not change.

**Complexity:** O(n log n) for the sort; O(1) extra space.

**Examples:**
- `[[1,2],[2,3],[3,4],[1,3]]` → `1` (remove `[1,3]`; the remaining three share only endpoints)
- `[[1,2],[1,2],[1,2]]` → `2` (only one copy can be kept)
- `[[1,2],[2,3]]` → `0` (endpoint-sharing does not count as overlap)
- `[]` → `0` (nothing to remove)
- `[[5,10]]` → `0` (single interval, always fine)
statement.es:
Dado `int[][] intervals` donde cada fila es `{start, end}` (ambos inclusivos), devuelve el **número mínimo de intervalos a eliminar** para que no queden dos intervalos solapados.

Firma: `public int eraseOverlapIntervals(int[][] intervals)`

**Definición de solapamiento:** Dos intervalos `[a, b]` y `[c, d]` se solapan cuando `c < b` (sus interiores se intersecan). Intervalos que comparten solo un extremo, como `[1, 2]` y `[2, 3]`, **NO** se solapan — el punto compartido es aceptable.

**Idea clave — greedy por tiempo de fin:** Ordena por `end` ascendente. Conserva con avaricia cada intervalo cuyo `start` sea ≥ al `end` del último intervalo conservado. Cada intervalo que no pueda conservarse debe eliminarse. Contar eliminaciones equivale a `n − (tamaño máximo del subconjunto sin solapamientos)`.

**Clave de ordenamiento:** `end` ascendente. El desempate en `end` es arbitrario: el conteo no cambia.

**Complejidad:** O(n log n) por la ordenación; O(1) espacio extra.

**Ejemplos:**
- `[[1,2],[2,3],[3,4],[1,3]]` → `1` (elimina `[1,3]`; los tres restantes comparten solo extremos)
- `[[1,2],[1,2],[1,2]]` → `2` (solo se puede conservar una copia)
- `[[1,2],[2,3]]` → `0` (compartir extremo no cuenta como solapamiento)
- `[]` → `0` (nada que eliminar)
- `[[5,10]]` → `0` (intervalo único, siempre válido)
